title: Korean Grammar for Absolute Beginners | Word Order & Core Particles (은/는, 이/가, 을/를)
description:
00:00 Ice Break (Skip)
02:10 Korean Word Order Practice 
20:10 Topic Particle 은/는
31:20 Subject Particle 이/가
49:58 Object Particle 을/를
01:14:40 Heads-up for next class

This class is for learners who are just starting Korean grammar! 📝

We’ll start from the very basics — like simple sentence structure and the core particles 은/는, 이/가 and 을/를, — and I’ll explain them in a way that’s easy to understand even if you’re just getting started. We’ll also practice making simple sentences together.

It’s recommended that you’re already somewhat familiar with Hangeul, the Korean alphabet; you don’t need to be 100% confident yet, but having a basic understanding of Korean consonants, vowels, and batchim will help. If you’ve never learned Hangeul at all, it might be a bit difficult to follow.

I'm excited about this class!🙌🏻✨

(📕The book I’m using on my iPad in this video is the companion workbook to my grammar book. It's titled "Korean Grammar Canvas for Beginners 1: Workbook," and it’s expected to be published this November. I’ll keep you guys updated when it’s released!)

title: Basic Korean 01 : Greeting, "I," "You," 반말(informal) vs 존댓말 (Formal)
description:
Hey guys! So I started wearing braces... as you might have noticed midway from the slurpiness here and there. It's hard for me to speak and pronounce some words correctly, both Korean and English. But, I tried my best.. sorta. Thank goodness I recorded at least some bits before having braces inside my mouth. If you have any questions or suggestion, leave them in the comments! I look forward to reading them. 
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••­­­•••••••••••
Connect with me here:
INSTAGRAM: http://instagram.com/explorekorean
WEBSITE : http://explorekorean.net
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••­­­•••••••••••
Review Sheet

Dialogue #1
Jiyeon: 안녕하세요 (Hello)
Jaeho: 안녕하세요 (Hello)
Jiyeon: 저는 이지연이에요. (I am Jiyeon Lee)
Jaeho: 저는 김재호입니다. 만나서 반갑습니다. (I am Jaeho Kim. Nice to meet you.)
Jiyeon: 네, 저도 만나서 반가워요 (Yes it's nice to meet you too.)

Dialogue #2
Jisoo: 안녕 (Hello)
Seoyoung: 안녕 (Hello)
Jisoo: 너는 이름이 뭐야? (What is your name?)
Seoyoung: 난 박서영이야. 넌? (I am Seoyoung Park. And you are?)
Jisoo: 난 이지수야. 너 정말 예쁘다 (I am Jisoo Lee. You are really pretty.)
Seoyoung: 고마워 (Thank you.)

Dialogue #3
Jisoo: 선생님 안녕하세요 (Hello, teacher.)
Teacher: 응 지수도 안녕. 잘 지내니? (Yes, hello to Jisoo too. Are you doing well?) 
Jisoo: 네, 선생님은요? (Yes, how about you?)

*Expressions

★Formal 존댓말
I am ___ (noun)
저는 ___ 입니다.
저는 ___ 이에요 (when the word ends with 받침)
저는 ___ 예요 (when the word ends without 받침)

저는 사람입니다. I'm a person.
저는 여자예요. I'm a woman.
저는 선생님이에요. I'm a teacher.

만나서 반갑습니다. 
만나서 반가워요
Nice to meet you!

★Casual 반말
I am ___ (noun)
나는 ___ 이야 (when the word ends with 받침)
나는 ___ 야  (when the word ends without 받침)

나는 사람이야. (I'm a person.)
나는 빅키야. (I'm Vicky)

너는 이름이 뭐야? (What is your name?)

나 I
너 you
이름 name
예쁘다 pretty

What's the word for "you" in formal form?
-Please watch the video to find out! 
(if you missed it, it starts from 06:00 in this vid)

#Korean #LearnKorean #KoreanLesson

title: 10+ Phrases for Beginners to Start Korean With
description:
[Summary Note]
1. Hello!
안녕하세요 (f)
안녕 (if)

2. Bye!
안녕히 가세요 (f) - used when the other person is leaving
안녕히 계세요  (f) - used when you are leaving 
안녕 (if)

3. Yes 
네 (f)
응 (if)

No
아니요(f)
아니(if)

4.Ok. (Got it)
알겠어요 (f)
알았어요 (f)

알겠어 (if)
알았어 (if)

5. Thank you.
감사합니다.(f)
고맙습니다.(f)
감사해요.(f)
고마워요.(f)

고마워(if)

6. Sorry.
죄송합니다 (f)
죄송해요 (f)
미안해요 (f)

미안해 (f)

7. Excuse me.
실례합니다 (but not used often, if not at all)

Alternative 1. 
잠시만요. (f)
지나갈게요 (f)

Alternative 2. 
저기요 (f)

I didn't put the informal form of excuse me, 
because the word "excuse me" already has a formal meaning, so the informal form isn't used often.

8. I am from _______.
저는 ______ 사람이예요. (f)

저는 미국 사람이예요.
저는 필리핀 사람이예요.
저는 인도 사람이예요.

(I didn't put it in the video but you can also say 사람입니다 instead of 사람이예요.)
저는 _______ 사람입니다. (also f)

나는 _______ 사람이야. (if)
나는 중국 사람이야.
나는 한국 사람이야

9. I can't speak Korean.
저 한국어 잘 못해요. (f)
저 한국어 잘 몰라요. (f)

나 한국어 잘 못해 (if)
나 한국어 잘 몰라 (if)

10. My name is _________.
제 이름은 _______ 예요. (f)
(제 이름은 ________ 입니다 is also another formal form I didn't mention.)

제 이름은 제프예요.(f)
제 이름은 레이첼이예요. (f)

내 이름은 _______야. (if)
내 이름은 빅키야. (if)
내 이름은 심효정이야. (if)

Watch the whole video for a detailed explanation. 화이팅!

title: 은/는 & 이/가 (Subject Marking Particles) Explained
description:
When to use 은/는 and 이/가 is one of what Korean learners find the most confusing to understand. Native Korean speakers can easily tell the difference, because we’ve grown up in this language our entire life, but we find it very difficult to explain this to non-natives. There are numerous contexts when using a language and it would be impossible to verbalize all of them, so I doubt there can be one single, clear answer to 은/는/이/가 confusion. But I do hope that the explanation and example sentences I have for you in this lesson help you inch closer to getting it. 화이팅, everyone! 💪

(If you want to skip right on to 은/는 & 이/가 comparison, you can start from 08:27 If you want to know what a particle and a subject marker is in Korean language, you're welcome to stay with me all the way from the beginning 😊) 

#은는이가 #KoreanParticles

title: Korean Numbers! (Sino & Native)
description:
Learn how to say numbers in Korean with this video, in both Sino and Native Korean! :)

e.g. 
[sino]
1 일 il 
2 이 ee 
3 삼  sam 
4 사 sa 
5 오 o 
6 육 yook 
7 칠 chil 
8 팔 pal 
9 구 goo

[native(pure)]
1 하나 hana
2 둘 dool
3 셋 set

title: How to say “I’m __ Years Old” in Korean
description:
Lesson on "how to say how old you are, and how to ask how old another person is in Korean" 

나이 (age) – Native Korean Number
저는 ___ 살이에요. (존댓말)
나는 ___ 살이야. (반말)

하나, 둘, 셋 넷 + MW = 한, 두, 세, 네

Birth year – Sino Korean Number
저는 ____ 년생이에요.

How old are you?
나이가 어떻게 되세요?
연세가 어떻게 되세요?
몇 살 이세요?
몇 살 이야?

title: Question Word 몇 & Counter Words (-개, -명, -마리, -시, -분, -달 and more)
description:
In this lesson, we learn how to use 몇 (how many) and counter words that can follow it. 몇 is used when you are asking for specific number of things, people or animals, and it can also be used when asking someone's age, what time it is, or how many hours/months it takes.

#몇 #KoreanCounterWord

title: How to Say "Because" in Korean
description:
This lesson might be a bit difficult to follow, so I tried to include many example sentences so that you can put into practice what we learned right away. Also, ‘ Verb + 서’ expression is used more often (in spoken Korean) than Verb + (이)기 때문에, so please don’t worry too much if you find verb conjugation with 때문에 a bit hard. 때문에 is more used in “Noun + 때문에 (blaming Noun)” expression, which I cover at the very last part of this lesson, so if you understand that one easily, it’s already a good progress :) 파이팅!!!

+) I forgot to include that you can also put “왜냐하면” before “-때문이에요” phrase. So when you are answering to the question “왜 벌써 퇴근해요?(Why get off work already?)” you can answer “몸이 안좋기 때문이에요” like we learned but also “왜냐하면 몸이 안좋기 때문이에요” works too. But you can’t say “왜냐하면 몸이 안좋기 때문에 일찍 퇴근해요(x)” because by putting 왜냐하면, you are already making it a full sentence. 왜냐하면 is pronounced (wae-nya-ha-myun). You can also just say 왜냐면 (wae-nya-myun) without 하. 

+) there’s also "verb+ -(으)니까" expression which means the same as verb+ –서, and we’ll cover that later

title: -(으)로's Many Usage (Korean Particle #2)
description:
🌸time stamp🌸
00:00 -(으)로's four main usage
01:08 -(으)로’s 받침 rule
03:00 First Usage : Direction
09:36 Second Usage : "to" a place (vs. -에)
19:03 Third Usage : A means of transportation & tool
27:21 Fourth Usage : Choice
35:32 Outro & About the Quiz

PARTICLES ARE HARD ㅠㅠ! I understand how challenging it is for you guys to take them all in, because even trying to explain these stuff as a native Korean speaker is not easy. I got lost several times midway trying to make it digestible to everyone. So please don’t worry too much if you can’t get all of them right away. I remember learning about the correct usage of English prepositions felt difficult as well, because one particle has several meanings. So, you’re not alone! Keep working hard and practice with as many sentences as you can until you get the hang of it. 파이팅!^^

title: Sentence Making Practice #2 (Friday Review Class)
description:
Live Class Streamed on July 31st 1pm (KST)

Hi guys! First of all, thank you so much for the heartfelt, supportive, and encouraging comments and private messages. I haven't had a chance to reply to all of them, but it made me feel so relieved and much better. I was honestly quite scared, but it feels good to know that a lot of you guys are so kind to understand. Thank you also for the heartfelt advice. I will take them to heart.

*Special thanks to @Robyn for helping me out as a moderator in today's class ♥

title: TOPIK 1 Listening Class (Live)
description:
Live Class Streamed on Sept 19th, 1pm (KST)

TOPIK 1 Test Sheet & Instruction
https://explorekorean.com/class-schedule/

If you haven't heard of TOPIK, check out this post:
https://explorekorean.com/2020/09/17/what-is-a-topik-test/

This class is for the listening section of the test, and Sept 20th class will be for the reading section. 

It is recommended that you finish the test and then join. We might not get to cover all 70 questions in class, but I’m aiming to do as many as possible. We'll see. Before class, you can request for the questions in the test that you’d like me to cover. Leave them here in live chat, and I’ll make sure to include the most requested ones.

title: Let's Play "Would You Rather" in Korean (밸런스 게임)
description:
[Class Notes(used in this class)]
Would You Rather Questions & Vocabs & Grammar Note : https://explorekorean.net/extra-class-notes/?vid=5

밸런스 게임(balance game) is a popular game in Korea, and basically similar to answering “Would you rather” questions. You are given two choices (of hypothetical situations), and you can only choose one of them. Each choice has its own benefits and drawbacks that are of perfect “balance” (thus the “balance” game), and that makes it a lot more difficult for you to make a final choice, which is supposed to be the whole point of this game haha. Some choices only have drawbacks, and it’s up to you to decide which one is better / less worse than the other! ㅎㅎ 

Let's play this game together and learn fun new Korean words, phrases and grammar at the same time. Before we start the game, we'll go over some of the grammar used in the questions/choices :) 
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••­­­•••••••••••

https://youtu.be/3qyUeNAwYH8 (냐고, 으라고, 자고)

title: Korean Grammar & Speaking Practice: -ㄹ/을 텐데, 테니까 (What's this "테"?)
description:
-ㄹ/을 텐데 and -ㄹ/을 테니까 are among the most frequently asked questions I get from intermediate students. Honestly, organizing the usage of -ㄹ/을 테 was a bit of a headache because it wasn't easy to come up with a definitive grammatical explanation for this. That’s why, instead of focusing on what’s absolutely correct from a grammatical perspective, I prioritized how these expressions are actually used in real-life conversations, like what’s more commonly heard in everyday speech. This video reflects the most common and practical usage of these expressions in modern spoken Korean :) So please keep this in mind as you learn! 

00:00 Intro
00:24 -ㄹ/을 테 vs. -ㄹ/을 거
02:08 -ㄹ/을 텐데
04:35 Quiz & Speaking Practice
06:32 -ㄹ/을 테니까
09:32 Speaking Practice
10:32 Recap
10:45 Outro

title: Learn Hangeul 한글 (Korean Alphabet) in 30 minutes
description:
00:00 Introduction to 한글
00:40 Consonants
06:41 Basic Vowels
11:12 Let’s read Korean words! #1
16:45 Breaktime & Encouragement
17:06 Compound Vowels (Diphthongs)
22:38 Let’s read Korean words! #2
24:54 Double Consonants
27:30 Final Consonants (Batchim)
35:55 Outro

To learn about Final Consonants in more detail, watch this video : https://youtu.be/yE6p6V7UpEY

For the entire Hangeul Playlist, check here : https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLECz2rpRD3Z0EeOU0z3aoafwgjHyth7MN&si=xLbVAqpoLstanrHR

For corrections and additional notes, please check the pinned comment in the comment section :)

📚 All-You-Need Korean for Absolute Beginners
Paperback: https://amzn.to/3zzB1Ot
eBook (full) : https://a.co/d/9N8ofjZ
eBook (Hangeul Chapter Only): https://a.co/d/g5kQu3q

To preview the book's content, feel free to watch this video:
https://youtu.be/nShFk5-HIZM?si=qJhufQtaX4hR0GNP
--------
If you have any questions, feel free to leave them in the comments below. 

Hope you found my class helpful, and do keep it up! 화이팅 (hwa i ting - it means cheers as an encouragement!)

Here's the review of the lesson:

자음 consonant 
14 basic consonant
ㄱ, ㄴ, ㄷ, ㄹ, ㅁ, ㅂ, ㅅ,
ㅇ, ㅈ, ㅊ, ㅋ, ㅌ, ㅍ, ㅎ
기역  ㄱ  g,  니은  ㄴ  n,
디귿  ㄷ  d,  리을 l
미음  ㅁ m, 비읍 ㅂ b
시옷  ㅅ  s,  이응  ㅇ  silent/ng,
지읒  ㅈ  j,   치읓  ㅊ ch, 
키읔 ㅋ k  티읕 ㅌ t,
피읖  ㅍ  p,  히읗  ㅎ h

모음 vowels,
ㅏa, ㅑya,  ㅓuh,  ㅕyuh,  ㅣi,
ㅗoh,  ㅛyoh,  ㅜooh,  ㅠyooh,  ㅡeu,

Vocabulary 
나비 butterfly,  도시 city,  하마 hippo,
나라 country,  소녀 a girl,  아기 baby, 
커피 coffee,  오이 cucumber,  우리  us/we, 
다리 bridge or leg, 

Vocabulary 2 
아니 no,   아니요 no,  바구니 basket,
버스 bus,  우유  milk,  이야기 story,
모자 hat/cap,  파스타 pasta,  라자냐 lasagna, 

Vowels 2
ㅐae,  ㅒyae,  ㅔae,  ㅖyae,
ㅚ wae,  ㅘ wa,  ㅙ wae,
ㅟ wi,  ㅝ wuh,  ㅞ wae,
ㅢ ui, 

Vocabulary 3,
애기 talk story,  새 bird,  네 yes (formal),
예쁘다, 왜,  과자 snack,
쉐이크 shake, 웨이터 waiter, 의자 chair,
위 up, 뒤 behind, 

쌍자음 double consonant, 
ㄲ Spanish g,  ㄸ Spanish d,  ㅃ p as in apple,
ㅆ s as i sun,  ㅉ zh(dj) as in 真(zhen) 

받침 Batchim (final consonant)
ㄱ 각 낙 막 박,  ㄴ 간 잔 칸 판,
ㄷ 닫 맏 믿,       ㄹ 갈 날 달 말,
ㅁ 감 남 담,       ㅂ 밥 삽 깁 흡,
ㅅ 깃 놋 닷 옷,  ㅇ 방탄소년단 BTS ,
ㅈ 갖 겆 짖,       ㅊ 읓 젗 닻 돛, 
ㅋ 해질녘 부엌, ㅍ same as ㅂ,
ㅎ 좋아  놓다,

쌍자음 
ㄲ 낚 삮  same as 닉 삭
ㅆ 했 있  same as 햇 잇

Vocabulary 4
대한민국 Republic of Korea
낚시 fishing 
돛단배 sailboat
믿음 belief, faith 
말굽 horse, hoof 
옷걸이 hanger 
방탄소년단 BTS 
지다 to bark
갖다 to have 
같다 same 
해질녘 sunset 
높다 high 
좋은사람 good person 
끝 the end 

잘했어요 good job! 

안녕 Bye (or hi)

title: All About 받침 (Batchim): Korean Final Consonants | ㄱ to ㅎ Reading Practice (Part#1)
description:
Here is the lesson on Consonant Assimilation
https://youtu.be/x3kwxK1b94I

For the entire Hangeul Playlist, check here : 
https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLECz2rpRD3Z0EeOU0z3aoafwgjHyth7MN&si=xLbVAqpoLstanrHR

📚 All-You-Need Korean for Absolute Beginners
Paperback: https://amzn.to/3zzB1Ot
eBook (full) : https://a.co/d/9N8ofjZ
eBook (Hangeul Chapter Only): https://a.co/d/g5kQu3q

To preview the book's content, feel free to watch this video:
https://youtu.be/nShFk5-HIZM?si=qJhufQtaX4hR0GNP
-------------------------------------------------------------------
This lesson would be difficult to follow if you haven't taken the basic 한글 (Hangul, Korean alphabets) lesson yet. In that case, you can start your first hangul lesson here:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=85qJXvyFrIc

받침 (batchim, aka the final consonant) is part of hangul that confuses learners the most. So I made separate lessons (part1, part2, part 3 - the other two will be up as well) to explain more in-depth about 받침. It's a long lesson because I've included a lot of read-it-together practice. If you find it too long, you can always speed up the video or skip to only the parts you need help with.

I'll see you in the next part 2 video (the link is at the top). We'll learn more about 자음동화 (consonant assimilation) a little of which we've also covered in this vid at ㅎ받침 section and at the very last bit.

title: Korean 겹받침 (Compound Final Consonants): ㄳ, ㄵ, ㄶ, ㄼ, ㄾ, ㅀ, ㅄ, ㄺ, ㄻ +
description:
In this lesson, we learn how to read Compound Final Consonants in Korean, or "Friend Consonants" as I term it :) 
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••­­­•••••••••••
Basics of 한글(Hangeul), the Korean Alphabet

title: All About 받침 Part 2 - Consonant Assimilation | Sound Like a Native Speaker
description:
You can check the updated video lesson on Consonant Assimilation (with more example vocabs and sentences) here: https://youtu.be/x3kwxK1b94I

Part 3 Lesson is up in the link below!
Reading Words with ㄳ, ㄵ, ㄶ, ㄼ, ㄾ, ㅀ, ㅄ, ㄺ, ㄻ as Final Consonants:
https://youtu.be/Qo-Tp409sKE
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••­­­•••••••••••
Video Description

받침 Part 2 - Here we learn how to read words and sentences where consonant assimilation (자음동화) happens! I know it gets trickier to understand in some parts, but as I always say, with more encounter and practice with various words and sentences, you'll get used to it and you'll also get to a level where you automatically make the correct pronunciation without even thinking.  Of course, that doesn't happen overnight, so I suggest you keep studying words and practice how to read them. Making sound while memorizing will help the words stick in your head a lot longer than otherwise as well :)


#Korean #FinalConsonant #Batchim

title: [Vocabulary Class] Hard-to-Translate Emotion Words in Korean
description:
Streamed July 19th, 9 pm (Korea Standard Time) 

02:10 Intro & Conjunction -서
05:48 One thing to note before we start
07:57 답답하다
14:01 민망하다
19:55 뻘쭘하다
24:28 섭섭하다
31:10 서운하다
34:55 아쉽다
39:13 시원하다
48:51 시원섭섭하다
52:10 속상하다
56:33 삐지다
01:02:53 서럽다
01:09:30 힘들다
01:15:54 Outro, Review Note & Thank you :)

We learn 12 emotion words in Korean, (답답하다, 민망하다, 뻘쭘하다, 섭섭하다, 서운하다, 아쉽다, 시원하다 / 후련하다, 시원섭섭하다, 속상하다, 삐지다, 서럽다, 힘들다) that are a bit hard to translate (or don't have the exact equivalent word) in English, and how they are used. We also learn many other commonly used vocabularies as we practice making sentences with them.

You can find the review note here:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gftxIhsMKU-CGXrekpMgxCoVRMKzpgLf/view?usp=sharing

It's the exact same file I used in this class :) 

Thank you again for joining~ 
I'll see you in the next lesson!

title: Let's learn Christmas-related Hanjas (한자)
description:
Merry Christmas, everyone! 여러분, 성탄절 즐겁게(merrily) 보내세요! :) I made this mini vocab session to help cool down our heads from hardcore grammar lessons, and learn something that we can study more lightly, takes less time & are easier to remember. You can consider this is my Christmas gift to you haha.‍🎄🎁 I hope you enjoy watching! 크리스마스에도 한국어 공부하실 분 있나요?ㅎㅎ

You can find the list of words/expressions we learned in this lesson here: https://explorekorean.net/extra-class-notes/?vid=4

channel_title: Learn Korean with GO! Billy Korean
title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #1: Course Introduction
description:
Welcome to your free Korean curriculum hosted by me, Billy Go. Yes, it’s really free (and always will be). This is a beginner level curriculum, and you don’t need to know anything about Korean to take your first steps into the language. This is the first introduction video which explains the course, as well as explains what you’ll learn and how you can get the most out of it. New videos will be posted to this channel weekly.

Want a textbook to start learning Korean? Check out my book, "Korean Made Simple" on Amazon: http://amzn.to/2bDBi6h (affiliate)

Here’s the full playlist for this course: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLbFrQnW0BNMUkAFj4MjYauXBPtO3I9O_k

Please consider supporting me on Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/gobillykorean

New merch is out! https://gobillykorean-shop.fourthwall.com/

Learn Korean with GoBillyKorean! Subscribe for weekly videos!

channel_title: Learn Korean with GO! Billy Korean
title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #2: Intro to 한글
description:
Welcome back! This lesson will introduce the Korean alphabet known as Hangul (한글). I’ll also talk about some of the downsides to using Romanization, and stroke order. Then you’ll learn our first few letters, and make your first syllables.

Want a textbook to start learning Korean? Check out my book, "Korean Made Simple" on Amazon: http://amzn.to/2bDBi6h (affiliate)

title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #9: Sound Change Rules for 한글
description:

channel_title: Learn Korean with GO! Billy Korean
title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #10: Hello and Goodbye
description:
Now that we’ve learned 한글 and all of the sound change rules, it’s time to start learning how to speak. This video introduces South Korea and the language known as 한국어, as well as sentence structure. Then you’ll learn how to say hello and goodbye, both to your close friends and to strangers.

You’ll also learn more about honorific verbs, humble verbs, honorific nouns, honorific particles, as well as humble nouns. title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #55: Comparisons
description:
In this lesson you’ll learn how to compare nouns to other nouns. You’ll learn the most basic way to make comparisons, by saying something is “more” or “less” than something else. You’ll also learn how to say that a noun is the same as, different than, or similar to another noun. And you’ll learn how to say that something is “like” a noun using both descriptive verbs and action verbs. Finally, you’ll learn how to say that a noun is the “most” or “best.”

In this lesson you’ll learn how to ask for clarification in a conversation. You’ll learn how to ask simple follow-up questions, as well as how to ask the other person to say something again, and how to ask someone to speak more slowly. And you’ll learn how to say that you understand, or didn’t understand something.

For example, you’ll learn how to count people (as well as when using Honorific Speech), animals, and general things. You’ll learn how to ask “how many” with nouns. Also you’ll learn how to make past tense verb stems, to use together with these forms and other forms.

In this lesson you’ll learn another useful verb ending, and sentence connector – the 데 form. You’ll learn how this form can be used to mean “but” or “and,” as well as how this form can be used to show contrast. And you’ll learn when to use this form, and how it differs from the other grammar forms we’ve already learned.

title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #73: If and When
description:
In this lesson you’ll learn two essential forms for saying “if” or “when.” These are the (으)면 form and the 때 form. One of the forms can translate as both “if” and “when” depending on the context, while the other form translates as only “when.” You’ll learn when to use each form, as well as (more importantly) when not to.

channel_title: Learn Korean with GO! Billy Korean
title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #76: Speaking With Friends
description:
In this lesson you’ll learn about casual speech (반말), and how you can speak Korean together with your closest friends. You’ll also get a quick overview of all the politeness levels we’ve learned so far. You’ll learn casual ways to refer to yourself and your friends, as well as how to say “you.” And you’ll learn how to say “yes” and “no” when using casual speech, along with how to use (or not use) the object marker. You’ll also learn casual ways to say “Let’s,” and how to make commands.

Want a textbook to start learning Korean? Check out my book, "Korean Made Simple" on Amazon: http://amzn.to/2bDBi6h (affiliate)

title: Billy Go’s Beginner Korean Course | #82: Making Adverbs
description:
In this lesson you’ll learn how you can change a descriptive verb into an adverb, using the 게 verb ending. You’ll also learn how descriptive verbs ending with 하다 can become adverbs as either 하게 or 히. And you’ll learn several more unique adverbs, to add to the ones we’ve already learned. Finally, you’ll learn how to use 재미(가) 있다 and 맛(이) 있다 as adverbs.

title: Learn Korean Language 1. Korean alphabet (consonant & vowel)
description:
We have Jenny's Korean course, and Emma's new Korean basic course is also being released this year in 2022. We recommend this new course. https://youtu.be/n6qXXoNjEZg

You can see the script for this lecture here. https://seemile.gumroad.com/l/Jenny_Korean_Class_PDF_Note 

List of Jenny's Korean (Mastering Korean in one month.) 
1. Korean alphabet & reading 한국어 자음, 모음, 읽기 https://youtu.be/0ZhOeA0RD9o
2. Korean alphabet 2 한국어 자음, 모음, 읽기 2 https://youtu.be/YFY1y6jxFcs
3. Self introduction, Nationality, Occupations 자기소개  https://youtu.be/Tt95XA3W6Fo
4. Location, places 위치, 장소, 있습니다. 없습니다. https://youtu.be/9F7-qBlWQFk
5. Verb, Action , 위치, 동작  https://youtu.be/f5UAcsUlAiA
6. Time, Numbers, 시간, 수 https://youtu.be/tU3Eg63b7dE
7. Activities, past expressions, 주말활동, 과거표현 https://youtu.be/q394uW4Aij4
8. Shopping, counting unit 쇼핑, 수량, 물건 값 표현  https://youtu.be/hMf4GQA39Rc
9. Day & Month, Date, Plan, Appointment 날짜, 약속, 요일 https://youtu.be/lddlrWPwajw
10. Season, Weather 날씨, 계절 https://youtu.be/2wLW50comVM
11. Family, happiness, Honorific expressions, 가족, 높임말 표현  https://youtu.be/dut3uKuEjnI
12. Thanks, Gratitude, Apologies, 감사, 사과  https://youtu.be/ZY8MeYNTG8M
13. Hobby, Leisure activity, Frequency adverbs 취미 https://youtu.be/dh8SIVYGUmw
14. Places, state, 장소, 상태  https://youtu.be/KEgDJUfe_OU
15.Health, body, symptom 건강, 신체부위, 증상 표현 https://youtu.be/SxTiplOWZt8
16. transportation, car, bus, subway, taxi, 교통수단  https://youtu.be/36RV9XfU1WE
17. Travel, Vacation, experiences 여행, 휴가, 경험 https://youtu.be/3F3QKHg-IKY
18. Appearance and Clothes expression 외모 https://youtu.be/HtRhZMbkYtk
19. Feels good, sad, Emotions, moods 감정 및 기분 https://youtu.be/XQI3YiNXJVQ
20."Letter" expression 편지 쓰기 https://youtu.be/5wMTrrkaVoU

Learning Korean is learning Korean culture. If you learn seemile Korean, you will understand Kpop lyrics without translation, and you will be able to watch Korean dramas and movies without subtitles. I understand and feel the songs of the Bulletproof Boys (BTS). Start learning with Korean with "seemile Korean".

The "seemile Team" also released app to learn Chinese (Mandarin).
→  "seemile Chinese" app android https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.seemile.chinese
